perturbation.original_text: Investor acknowledges that it is aware, and that it will advise its Representatives who receive the Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non-public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto) or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Investor acknowledges that it is aware, and that it will advise its Representatives who receive the Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non-public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto) or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. Investor also acknowledges that they may solicit the Company for employment opportunities after the agreement expires.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the insider trading warning to a section dealing with general acknowledgements, instead of a section dedicated to compliance, lessens its impact. By adding an unrelated sentence, it becomes easier to overlook.
perturbation.law_explanation: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 § 10(b) and Rule 10b-5 require companies to adequately disclose insider trading prohibitions to prevent misuse of material non-public information. Placing the acknowledgement in a less conspicuous section and burying it behind other information reduces the prominence of this disclosure, making it harder to prove that the investor was properly informed of and understood the restrictions, especially since employment solicitation is unrelated.

Manipulative and deceptive devices It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce or of the mails, or of any facility of any national securities exchange- (a)(1) To effect a short sale, or to use or employ any stop-loss order in connection with the purchase or sale, of any security other than a government security, in contravention of such rules and regulatio
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the prominence of the insider trading disclosure, making it potentially easier to overlook. While it doesn't remove the disclosure, placing it in a section dealing with general acknowledgements, along with an unrelated sentence about employment opportunities, lessens its impact, potentially undermining compliance with securities laws. The Law Explanation states this reduces the prominence of the disclosure. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing 15 USC 78j, addresses manipulative and deceptive devices, suggesting that inadequate disclosure or burying important information could be construed as a deceptive practice related to securities exchanges. This supports the assessment that the change creates a legal contradiction by potentially undermining the effectiveness of the insider trading warning.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without reference to its conflicts of law principles. Each party consents to the jurisdiction of the state or federal courts in Cook County, Illinois, which will be the sole venue for resolution of all disputes related to this Agreement. THE PARTIES HERETO WAIVE THE RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN CONNECTION WITH ANY MATTER ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without reference to its conflicts of law principles. Each party consents to the jurisdiction of the state or federal courts in Cook County, Illinois, which will be the sole venue for resolution of all disputes related to this Agreement. However, in the event that the agreement is found unenforceable, each party may seek remedy in a mutually agreed location.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the jury trial waiver and replacing it with a clause about alternative resolution lessens its impact. By changing it, the lack of jury trial agreement becomes easier to overlook.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Illinois Constitution guarantees the right to a jury trial. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Removing the explicit waiver of jury trial and instead mentioning a 'mutually agreed location' introduces ambiguity and potentially undermines the validity of a jury trial waiver, if one was intended.

Illinois Constitution From Ballotpedia Jump to: navigation , search Illinois Constitution Preamble Articles I • II • III • IV • V • VI • VII • VIII • IX • X • XI • XII • XIII • XIV • Schedule Contents 1 Features 2 Background 3 Preamble 4 Article I 5 Article II 6 Article III 7 Article IV 8 Article V 9 Article VI 10 Article VII 11 Article VIII 12 Article IX 13 Article X 14 Article XI 15 Article XII 16 Article XIII 17 Article XIV 18 Schedule 19 Amending the constitution 19.1 Initiative 19.2 Legislature 19.3 Convention 20 See also 21 External links 22 Footnotes The Illinois Constitution is the state constitution of Illinois . The current Illinois Constitution was adopted in 1970. Illinois has had four state constitutions. The current state constitution has 14 articles. The current Illinois Constitution has been amended 15 times . [1] Voters last approved a new amendment to the Illinois Constitution on November 8, 2022 , when voters approved Amendment 1 . A state constitution is the fundamental document that outlines a state's framework for governance, including the powers, structure, and limitations of the state government, individual and civil rights, and other matters. Features The 1970 Constitution of Illinois has a preamble, 14 articles and a schedule. Article XIV requires that Illinois voters be asked at least every 20 years if they desire a constitutional convention . The last time that measure was put to a vote was in 2008 . The ballot measure failed with 67 percent voting against the measure. [2] Background Illinois became the 21st state on December 3, 1818. The first Illinois Constitution was adopted in 1818, when Illinois was admitted to the Union. Constitutional revisions were subsequently ratified in 1848, 1870 and 1970. [3] In 1862, a constitutional convention was held, but the changes known as the "Copperhead constitution" were not ratified by the voters. [4] A constitutional convention was held in 1920, but in 1922 the changes were rejected by voters. [5] The last time Illinois residents were asked if they desired to hold a constitutional convention was in 2008 . The ballot measure failed with 67 percent voting against the measure. [2] Preamble See also: Preamble, Illinois Constitution and Preambles to state constitutions The preamble to the Illinois Constitution states: We, the People of the State of Illino
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an explicit waiver of the right to a jury trial, which is a legally protected right. The changed text removes this explicit waiver and replaces it with a clause about alternative resolution in a mutually agreed location if the agreement is found unenforceable. This alteration creates ambiguity and lessens the impact of the waiver. The law explanation states that the Illinois Constitution guarantees the right to a jury trial, which can be waived, but the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Removing the explicit waiver and introducing ambiguity potentially undermines the validity of the waiver.
